headline: Can I Deal with Storm Damage Myself?
articleBody:
After a storm, it’s completely natural to want to jump in and start cleaning up the mess yourself. You might think, 'I can just sweep up this water or patch that small leak.' However, taking on storm damage yourself can often lead to bigger problems down the road, especially if you're not equipped with the right tools or expertise. For instance, what looks like a minor leak can hide more extensive water intrusion into walls and subfloors, setting the stage for mold.

One common mistake we see is people failing to properly secure their property. Not tarping a damaged roof or boarding up broken windows immediately means you're inviting more water, potential vandalism, or even critters into your home. And while you might think a few household fans will dry everything out, professional dehumidification equipment is crucial to adequately dry out all affected building materials and prevent lingering moisture issues.

Incomplete documentation for insurance is another pitfall many property owners encounter. You need detailed photos and an itemized list of damages, something a professional team does as a standard part of their process. They understand what insurers require and can help streamline the claims process. Ultimately, if there’s anything more than surface-level damage, or if moisture has been present for over 24-48 hours, professional intervention becomes really important. Their trained assessment, specialized equipment, and knowledge of local building codes for repairs make a huge difference in getting your property truly restored.

Instead of risking further damage or inadequate repairs, contact a trusted restoration company promptly after a storm. We're here to help you get back on your feet.
